stemHtml: The French Safety Board (BEA) published a preliminary report of a serious incident involving an Airbus A330 being operated as a commercial air transport flight at Sydney, Australia. The Report states: "During take-off, a section of engine cowling from the NO. 1 engine separated from the aircraft. The crew conducted a fuel dump and returned the aircraft to Sydney." The incident did NOT involve an engine fire. What is the most appropriate explanation for why the crew dumped fuel after take-off?​

options.a: They made a mistake: According to normal procedures, the aircraft should have landed with all the remaining usable fuel.
options.b: They had to mitigate against the risk of fire and therefore empty the fuel tanks as much as possible.​
options.c: They had to reduce the aircraft's mass in order to safely land below the maximum landing mass.
options.d: After take-off, it is a normal procedure to dump some fuel in order to reduce pollution.
